name: Lavinia
description: Lavinia, in Roman mythology, was the daughter of King Latinus and Amata. Destined to marry a foreign hero, her fate was intertwined with the arrival of Aeneas, a Trojan prince who had fled the fall of Troy. Her mother, Amata, favored Turnus, king of the Rutulians, as a suitor for Lavinia, setting the stage for a fierce war between the Trojans and the Rutulians. Lavinia was depicted as a woman of great beauty and virtue, though often portrayed as a passive figure, a prize to be won. The prophecy concerning her marriage to a foreigner was central to the conflict, as it dictated the future of the land and its people. Her eventual marriage to Aeneas fulfilled the prophecy and solidified the Trojan presence in Italy. After Aeneas' death, Lavinia ruled alongside her stepson Ascanius, the founder of Alba Longa, a city from which Romulus and Remus, the founders of Rome, would later descend. Thus, Lavinia is considered a key ancestral figure in the founding of the Roman civilization, embodying the fusion of Trojan and Latin cultures and the fulfillment of divine destiny. She represents the promise of a new beginning and the establishment of a powerful and enduring legacy. Her story, primarily narrated in Virgil's Aeneid, underscores the importance of fate, duty, and the establishment of Roman identity. Lavinia's role, though seemingly passive, is crucial to the narrative, as her marriage to Aeneas is the catalyst for the birth of the Roman race.
subtitle: The mother of the Roman people.
achievements: Fulfilling the prophecy of marrying a foreign hero
Bearing a son who would found a new dynasty
Ruling alongside Ascanius after Aeneas' death
Becoming a key ancestor of the Roman people

keyMyths.title: The Prophecy of Marriage
keyMyths.description: Lavinia was prophesied to marry a foreigner, a hero who would bring great fortune to the land. This prophecy fueled the conflict between Turnus and Aeneas for her hand in marriage.

keyMyths.title: The War with Turnus
keyMyths.description: Turnus, Lavinia's betrothed, challenged Aeneas for her hand, leading to a bloody war between the Trojans and the Rutulians. This conflict was pivotal in establishing Trojan dominance in Italy.

keyMyths.title: The Founding of Lavinium
keyMyths.description: After Aeneas' death, Lavinia ruled alongside Ascanius and is credited with or associated with the founding of Lavinium. Lavinium was a key city in the early history of Latium.

sources.author: Virgil
sources.description: The primary source for the story of Lavinia and Aeneas.
sources.text: The primary source for the story of Lavinia and Aeneas.

sources.author: Livy
sources.description: A Roman history that mentions Lavinia's role in the founding of Rome.
sources.text: A Roman history that mentions Lavinia's role in the founding of Rome.
